 {"id":"bd-1vpz","title":"fastapi-openapi: Complete OpenAPI 3.1 Support","description":"Complete fastapi-openapi with all type schemas, constraints, security schemes, and proper $ref handling.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:55:05.979680191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:31:32.483368646Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:31:32.483305067Z","close_reason":"Complete OpenAPI 3.1 support:
- Full spec types: OpenApi, Info, Contact, License, Server, PathItem, Operation, Parameter, RequestBody, Response
- Schema system: Schema enum with all types, constraints, format, enum, nullable
- SchemaRegistry: Proper $ref handling with deduplication
- Security: ApiKey, Http, OAuth2, OpenIdConnect schemes
- Components: schemas and security_schemes
- OpenApiBuilder: Fluent API for document construction
- 25 tests passing","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":18,"issue_id":"bd-1vpz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Overview: Complete OpenAPI 3.1 Support

This epic groups all work to generate complete, valid OpenAPI 3.1 documentation from the type system.

## Current State
Basic OpenAPI types exist but:
- Missing JsonSchema for numeric primitives
- Enum schema generation incomplete
- Constraints not serialized
- No proper $ref handling (schemas inlined)
- Security schemes not generated
- Missing path parameter documentation

## Child Tasks (in suggested order)
1. **bd-12f8** [P1] - JsonSchema for numeric types (foundational)
2. **bd-3i50** [P1] - Schema constraints serialization (blocked by bd-12f8)
3. **bd-176a** [P1] - Components and $ref support
4. **bd-2p48** [P1] - Path parameters in OpenAPI doc
5. **bd-2q1r** [P1] - Request body schema generation
6. **bd-2n7h** [P2] - Security scheme definitions
7. **bd-2ta3** [P2] - Tag grouping for routes
8. **bd-1csj** [P2] - Response examples generation

## Critical Path for Enum Support
bd-12f8 -> bd-20ff (enum schema in macros)

Numeric types must have schemas before enums can use them for discriminants.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All primitive types have JsonSchema impl
- [ ] Struct and enum schemas generated correctly
- [ ] Validation constraints in schema (min, max, pattern)
- [ ] Proper $ref usage (no duplicate schemas)
- [ ] Security schemes documented
- [ ] Generated spec validates with OpenAPI validators

## OpenAPI 3.1 Compliance Notes
- JSON Schema draft 2020-12 alignment
- `nullable` replaced by `type: ["string", "null"]`
- `$ref` can have siblings
- `discriminator` for polymorphic types

 {"id":"bd-1wmx","title":"Router: Mount sub-routers with prefix","description":"Support mounting a Router at a path prefix: router.mount('/api/v1', api_router). Essential for API versioning and modular route organization.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:57:11.756568730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:07:31.851778579Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T05:07:31.851677932Z","close_reason":"Implemented sub-router mounting with mount() and nest() methods. Added 14 comprehensive tests. All 102 tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1wmx","depends_on_id":"bd-fhmy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:57:59.677644720Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":12,"issue_id":"bd-1wmx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Background & Context
Sub-router mounting is essential for organizing large APIs. It allows defining routes in modules and mounting them at prefixes.

## Current Limitation
Router has no mount() method. All routes must be added directly to root router.

## Solution Architecture

### API Design
```rust
let api_v1 = Router::new()
    .route("/users", get_users)
    .route("/items", get_items);

let admin = Router::new()
    .route("/stats", get_stats);

let app = App::new()
    .mount("/api/v1", api_v1)  // All routes under /api/v1/
    .mount("/admin", admin);   // All routes under /admin/
```

### Implementation
Mount merges child routes into parent trie at prefix:
```rust
impl Router {
    pub fn mount(mut self, prefix: &str, child: Router) -> Self {
        for route in child.routes {
            let full_path = format!("{}{}", prefix.trim_end_matches('/'), route.path);
            self.add(route.method, &full_path, route.handler);
        }
        self
    }
}
```

### Considerations
1. Trailing slashes: `/api/v1` + `/users` = `/api/v1/users`
2. Middleware inheritance: Child routes may want parent middleware
3. Path parameter conflicts: Prefixes shouldn't shadow child params
4. Performance: Don't duplicate trie structure, integrate into parent

## Files to Modify
- crates/fastapi-router/src/lib.rs - Router::mount()
- May need route prefix tracking for middleware

## Dependencies
- None for basic mount
- bd-3s9z (route metadata) for middleware inheritance

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Router.mount(prefix, child) method exists
- [ ] Child routes accessible at prefix + child path
- [ ] Path parameters work in both prefix and child
- [ ] Nested mounts work (mount router that has mounts)
- [ ] Tests verify mounting behavior

 {"id":"bd-20ff","title":"CRITICAL: Enum schema generation in derive macros","description":"The JsonSchema derive macro doesn't properly generate OpenAPI schemas for Rust enums. Enum variants should map to oneOf/anyOf in OpenAPI 3.1. This blocks API documentation for any enum types.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:49:08.904417326Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:43:48.584481586Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:43:35.446078674Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20ff","depends_on_id":"bd-12f8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:54:37.159068059Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-20ff","depends_on_id":"bd-1t3y","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:56:10.805381065Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"bd-20ff","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Background & Context
The JsonSchema derive macro generates OpenAPI 3.1 schemas from Rust types. While it works for structs, enum support is incomplete or missing. Rust enums should map to OpenAPI oneOf/anyOf constructs.

## Current Problem
```rust
#[derive(JsonSchema)]
enum Status {
    Active,
    Inactive,
    Pending { reason: String },
}
```
This should generate:
```json
{
  "oneOf": [
    { "const": "Active" },
    { "const": "Inactive" },
    {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "Pending": {
          "type": "object",
          "properties": { "reason": { "type": "string" } }
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

But the macro either:
1. Doesn't handle enums at all
2. Generates incorrect schemas
3. Panics on enum input

## Enum Variants to Support

### Unit variants
```rust
enum Color { Red, Green, Blue }
// -> "enum": ["Red", "Green", "Blue"]
```

### Tuple variants
```rust
enum Result { Ok(T), Err(E) }
// -> oneOf with type discrimination
```

### Struct variants
```rust
enum Message { Text { body: String }, Image { url: String, width: u32 } }
// -> oneOf with object schemas
```

### Mixed variants
Real-world enums often mix variant types

## Solution Architecture

### In fastapi-macros/src/lib.rs
1. Add enum handling to JsonSchema derive
2. Parse enum variants
3. Generate appropriate schema based on variant type
4. Handle #[serde(rename_all)] and other serde attributes
5. Support #[schema()] attributes for customization

### Schema Generation Strategy
```rust
fn generate_enum_schema(enum_data: &DataEnum) -> TokenStream {
    let variants: Vec<_> = enum_data.variants.iter().map(|v| {
        match &v.fields {
            Fields::Unit => generate_const_schema(&v.ident),
            Fields::Unnamed(f) => generate_tuple_schema(&v.ident, f),
            Fields::Named(f) => generate_struct_schema(&v.ident, f),
        }
    }).collect();
    
    quote! {
        schemars::schema::Schema::Object(schemars::schema::SchemaObject {
            subschemas: Some(Box::new(schemars::schema::SubschemaValidation {
                one_of: Some(vec![#(#variants),*]),
                ..Default::default()
            })),
            ..Default::default()
        })
    }
}
```

## Files to Modify
1. crates/fastapi-macros/src/lib.rs - Main macro implementation
2. crates/fastapi-macros/src/schema.rs - Schema generation helpers (may need to create)
3. crates/fastapi-openapi/src/lib.rs - Ensure Schema types support oneOf

## Dependencies
- Requires understanding of serde's enum serialization rules
- Should align with schemars crate conventions where possible

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit variant enums generate correct schema
- [ ] Tuple variant enums generate correct schema
- [ ] Struct variant enums generate correct schema
- [ ] Mixed variant enums generate correct schema
- [ ] #[serde(tag = "type")] adjacently tagged enums work
- [ ] #[serde(untagged)] untagged enums work
- [ ] Generated schemas validate in OpenAPI validators
- [ ] Tests cover all enum patterns

## Test Plan
1. Unit test: Each variant type in isolation
2. Integration test: Enums with serde attributes
3. Validation test: Schemas pass OpenAPI validator
4. Round-trip test: Schema correctly describes enum serialization

## Considerations
- Serde has multiple tagging strategies (external, internal, adjacent, untagged)
- Each produces different JSON, requiring different schemas
- Should match serde's actual behavior exactly
- Consider generating discriminator hints for code generators

## Implementation
- Test Request scope: new instance per request
- Test Singleton scope: same instance across requests
- Test Transient scope: new instance every time
- Test caching behavior per scope

## Test Cases
1. Request scope creates one instance per request
2. Request scope reuses within same request
3. Singleton scope shares across requests
4. Transient scope creates new each time
5. Scope interacts correctly with cleanup
